market_summary: Global markets experiencing volatility due to Iran conflict, inflation, and Fed's hawkish stance. Conversely, the art market shows resilience with high-profile museum exhibitions (MoMA, de Young, New Museum) and a clear 'flight to quality' among collectors.
art_insights: The market is stabilizing. Collectors are prioritizing provenance and high-value physical assets over speculative trends, despite the broader economic uncertainty. Recent updates include a shift in art briefing format to include detailed news summaries and direct links.
key_events: Market volatility: Iran conflict, inflation, Fed higher-for-longer signal
Exhibition spotlight: Frida and Diego (MoMA), Monet/Venice (de Young), New Humans (New Museum)
Art sector sentiment: Cautiously optimistic, focus on long-term value
last_update_note: Automated sync: Market update with Art Briefing format policy update.
